about: Union Bank of India is engaged in the Business of Banking Services, Government Business ,Merchant Banking, Agency Business Insurance, Mutual Funds, Wealth Management etc.[1]

key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

Union Bank of India is engaged in the Business of Banking Services, Government Business ,Merchant Banking, Agency Business Insurance, Mutual Funds, Wealth Management etc.[1]

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Segments Revenue Q3 FY26[1]

1) Corporate and Wholesale Banking (33%): The Bank offers a variety of products, including working capital loans, term loans, and project financing to corporate clients, businesses, and large enterprises.[2]

2) Retail Banking (~38%): The Bank provides a variety of retail loans, including home loans, vehicle loans, and personal loans, to meet the diverse needs of its customers. [3]

3) Treasury (23%): The Bank’s treasury operations involve managing the Bank’s liquidity, investments, and foreign exchange activities. The Bank employs advanced risk management techniques to ensure optimal liquidity and maximize ROI. [4]

4) Other Banking Operations (~6%): Offers digital banking services, including app banking, internet banking, self-service banking, ATM banking, SMS banking, etc. [5] [6]

Market Share[7]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank’s market share in deposits and net advances is ~5% and 5.1%, respectively, thereby making it the fifth-largest public sector bank.

Key Ratios
Capital Adequacy Ratio - 16.49% in Q3 FY26 vs 14.5% in FY22 [8][9]
NIM - 2.76% in Q3 FY26 vs 2.9% in FY22 [10]

Improvement in Asset Quality[11]
Gross NPA - 3.06% in Q3 FY26 vs 11.11% in FY22
Net NPA - 0.51% in Q3 FY26 vs 3.68% in FY22
PCR - 95.13% in Q3 FY26 vs 83.6% in FY22[12]
The bank has employed a multi-faceted approach to recover and resolve NPAs. Key strategies include- Legal Resources like SARFAESI and DRT Acts for quick recovery, Restructuring of NPA Accounts, and One-Time Settlement. [13]

Branch Network[14]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank has a network of 8,671 Branches & 8,300 ATMs. It has overseas branches in Sydney and Dubai, along with operations in London through WOS, Union Bank of India (UK) Ltd., and a JV in Malaysia, India International Bank (Malaysia) Berhad. [15]
Rural: 30%
Semi-Urban: 29%
Metro: 21%
Urban: 20% [16]

Loan Book
As of Q3 FY26, Gross advances stood at Rs. ~10,16,884 Cr vs Rs. ~7,16,400 Cr in FY22.
Corporate & Other: 43%
Retail: ~24%
Agriculture: 17%
MSME: 15%
[17][18]

Loan Book Exposure
Infrastructure- ~10%, NBFC& HFC- 15%, Food Processing- 0.5%, Basic Metals- 2.7%, Textiles- 1.7%, Chemical - 1.4%, Petroleum- 1.8%.
Total exposure to these industries stood at ~33% of domestic advances in Q3 FY26 down from 43% as of FY22. [19][20]

Deposits
As of Q3 FY26, total deposits stood at Rs. ~12,22,856 Cr vs Rs. ~10,32,300 Cr in FY22.
CASA: ~34% in Q3 FY26 vs 36% in FY22
Retail Term Deposit: 45%
Bulk Term Deposit: 21%[21][22]

Subsidiaries[23]
The group operates through multiple subsidiaries, joint ventures, and an associate to expand financial services and geographic reach. Wholly owned subsidiaries include Union Bank of India (UK) Ltd. supporting booking business in the UK and Europe, Union Asset Management Co. Pvt. Ltd. managing AUM of ~₹25,822 crore, UBI Services Ltd. sourcing retail and MSME loans across India for the parent bank, Andhra Bank Financial Services Ltd. undertaking leasing, hire purchase and merchant banking activities, and Union Trustee Co. Pvt. Ltd. acting as trustee for Union Mutual Fund.

Joint Ventures[23]
Key joint ventures include Star Union Dai-ichi Life Insurance Co. Ltd. (25.1% stake), a fast-growing private life insurer with 171 branches and participation in PMJJBY covering ~1.27 crore lives, and ASREC India Ltd. (26.02% stake) focused on improving NPA resolution through DRT processes. The bank also holds a 35% stake in Andhra Pradesh Grameena Bank, which serves rural financial needs across districts in Andhra Pradesh.

Merger
In 2020, Andhra Bank and Corporation Bank were amalgamated into the Union Bank of India. This merger has enhanced UBI’s operational capabilities, customer reach, and financial strength, positioning. [24]

Outlook[25]
The bank’s official stance for FY26 is to defend the current NIM level of 2.76%. However, leadership expressed optimism for achieving a better result than this target.
